# DVT Awesome Skills

> Internal, tool-agnostic collection of DVT's reusable AI skills and coding instructions.
> **Author once → generate for GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf, Claude Code, and Kiro.**

<!-- This file is generated by `npm run build`. Edit canonical assets in `skills/` and `instructions/`, not here. -->

## Getting started

```bash
git clone https://github.com/bassonrichard/dvt-awesome-skills.git
cd dvt-awesome-skills
npm install

npm run build        # regenerate README.md, llms.txt, and website data
npm run website:dev  # browse skills + instructions locally
```

## Install into your project

**Option A — GitHub CLI** (requires GitHub CLI >= v2.90.0):

```bash
gh skill install bassonrichard/dvt-awesome-skills <skill-name> --agent <github-copilot|cursor|windsurf|claude-code|kiro-cli>
```

**Option B — git clone** (works on any git host, no extra tooling). Pre-built native
files live in `installs/<tool>/<skill-name>/`; run from your project root:

```bash
git clone --no-checkout --depth 1 https://github.com/bassonrichard/dvt-awesome-skills.git .dvt-skill-tmp \
  && git -C .dvt-skill-tmp sparse-checkout set --no-cone installs/<tool>/<skill-name> \
  && git -C .dvt-skill-tmp checkout \
  && cp -R .dvt-skill-tmp/installs/<tool>/<skill-name>/. . \
  && rm -rf .dvt-skill-tmp
```

`<tool>` is one of `copilot`, `cursor`, `windsurf`, `claude`, `kiro`. For a Claude Code
user-scope install, copy into `~` instead of `.`. The `installs/` tree is generated by
`npm run build` — don't edit it by hand.

Browse everything on the [internal website](website/) (`npm run website:dev`).

## 🎯 Skills (3)

Self-contained capabilities with progressive-disclosure references.

| Skill | Description | Tools |
|---|---|---|
| [`dvt-adr-author`](skills/dvt-adr-author/SKILL.md) | Use when capturing an architecture decision at DVT. Produces a consistent Architecture Decision Record (ADR) with context, options, decision, and consequences. | copilot, cursor, windsurf, claude, kiro |
| [`dvt-new-service-scaffold`](skills/dvt-new-service-scaffold/SKILL.md) | Use when creating a new backend service at DVT. Scaffolds the standard service layout, health checks, structured logging, and CI wiring so every service starts from the same baseline. | copilot, cursor, windsurf, claude, kiro |
| [`dvt-secure-api-review`](skills/dvt-secure-api-review/SKILL.md) | Use when reviewing a DVT REST or GraphQL API against the company security baseline before release. Walks authn/authz, input validation, rate limiting, secrets, and logging. | copilot, cursor, windsurf, claude, kiro |

## 📋 Instructions (3)

Coding standards applied by activation mode (always / file pattern / model-requested / manual).

| Instruction | Description | Applies | Tools |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`git-pr-conventions`](instructions/git-pr-conventions.instructions.md) | DVT git and pull request conventions that always apply across repositories | Always on | copilot, cursor, windsurf, claude, kiro |
| [`security-baseline`](instructions/security-baseline.instructions.md) | DVT security baseline — load when handling auth, secrets, user input, or data access | Model-requested | copilot, cursor, windsurf, claude, kiro |
| [`typescript-standards`](instructions/typescript-standards.instructions.md) | DVT TypeScript coding standards for application and service code | `**/*.ts, **/*.tsx` | copilot, cursor, windsurf, claude, kiro |

## Contributing

```bash
npm run skill:create -- --name my-skill
npm run instruction:create -- --name my-standard --activation glob --globs "**/*.ts"
npm run validate    # CI gate
npm run build       # regenerate this file + llms.txt + website data
```

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) and [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md).
